subtopics.title: Introduction to Complex Numbers
common_misconceptions: The conjugate of a+bi is -a-bi (NO, it's a-bi)
subtopics.teaching_note: Definition: i = √(-1), so i² = -1. Complex number: z = a + bi where a = Re(z), b = Im(z). Conjugate: z̄ = a - bi. Modulus: |z| = √(a² + b²). Argument: θ = tan⁻¹(b/a). Argand diagram: complex plane with real axis (x) and imaginary axis (y). z = a + bi = r(cos θ + i sin θ) = re^(iθ) where r = |z|.

subtopics.title: Operations with Complex Numbers
common_misconceptions: (a+bi)(a-bi) = a² + b² (it's a² + b², but without i)
Division is done by dividing real and imaginary parts separately
subtopics.teaching_note: Addition: (a+bi) + (c+di) = (a+c) + (b+d)i. Multiplication: FOIL, remember i² = -1. Division: multiply numerator and denominator by conjugate of denominator. De Moivre's theorem: [r(cos θ + i sin θ)]ⁿ = rⁿ(cos nθ + i sin nθ). Roots: z^(1/n) = r^(1/n)[cos((θ+2πk)/n) + i sin((θ+2πk)/n)] for k = 0, 1, ..., n-1.

subtopics.title: Matrix Operations
common_misconceptions: Matrix multiplication is commutative (NO, AB ≠ BA generally)
All matrices can be multiplied (orders must match: m×n × n×p)
A zero matrix has no purpose
subtopics.teaching_note: Matrix multiplication: for AB, A must have same number of columns as B has rows. (m×n) × (n×p) = (m×p). Element (i,j) of AB = row i of A · column j of B (dot product). Identity matrix I: AI = IA = A. Transpose: A^T swaps rows and columns. Properties: (AB)^T = B^T A^T, (A+B)^T = A^T + B^T.

subtopics.title: Determinants and Inverse Matrices
common_misconceptions: If det(A) = 0, A = 0 (NO, matrix is singular but not zero)
All matrices have inverses (only non-singular square matrices)
subtopics.teaching_note: 2×2 determinant: |a b| = ad - bc. |c d|. 3×3: expansion by cofactors. Inverse: A⁻¹ = (1/det(A)) × adj(A). A⁻¹ exists iff det(A) ≠ 0. Properties: det(AB) = det(A)det(B), det(A^T) = det(A), det(kA) = kⁿ det(A) for n×n matrix. Cramer's rule for solving linear systems: x = det(Aₓ)/det(A), y = det(Aᵧ)/det(A).

subtopics.title: Vector Algebra
common_misconceptions: a · b = a × b (NO, dot product is scalar, cross product is vector)
Two vectors are equal if they have same magnitude (need same direction too)
subtopics.teaching_note: Vector: a = (a₁, a₂, a₃) = a₁i + a₂j + a₃k. Magnitude: |a| = √(a₁² + a₂² + a₃²). Unit vector: â = a/|a|. Dot product: a · b = |a||b|cos θ = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂ + a₃b₃. Cross product: a × b = (a₂b₃-a₃b₂, a₃b₁-a₁b₃, a₁b₂-a₂b₁). |a × b| = |a||b|sin θ = area of parallelogram. Perpendicular: a · b = 0. Parallel: a × b = 0.

subtopics.title: Parabola, Ellipse and Hyperbola
common_misconceptions: All conics are circles (circle is special case of ellipse)
e > 1 for ellipse (NO, e < 1 for ellipse)
Parabola has two foci (NO, one focus and one directrix)
subtopics.teaching_note: General conic: ax² + 2hxy + by² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0. Eccentricity e: parabola e=1, ellipse e<1, hyperbola e>1, circle e=0. Parabola: y² = 4ax, focus (a,0), directrix x = -a. Ellipse: x²/a² + y²/b² = 1, foci (±ae, 0), directrices x = ±a/e. Hyperbola: x²/a² - y²/b² = 1, asymptotes y = ±(b/a)x. Latus rectum: chord through focus perpendicular to axis. Circle: x² + y² = r².
